name: app.R
content:
library(shiny)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(hrbrthemes)
library(transformr)
library(janitor)
library(gifski)
library(shinydashboard)
library(DT)
library(htmltools)
library(bslib)
library(shinyjs)
library(fresh)
library(plotly)
library(ggokabeito)


#Here I add the password protection
app_password <- "besteverfertilitydatabasecr8ed!"


#Read in all the data; 
load(file.path("data", "childlessness with gam model with data source and HFD changes.RData"))
load(file.path("data", "agefb.RData"))
load(file.path("data", "asfrdhs.RData"))

smoothed_data_all_ds_hfd$parameter <- "Childlessness"

#Create the dataset for the sources with the respective country name in childlessness
ds_g_country_flag <- smoothed_data_all_ds_hfd %>%
  mutate(Data_Source = case_when(Data_Source == "Survey_National Statistics" ~ "National Statistics surveys",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_WFS" ~ "WFS",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_FFS" ~ "FFS",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_CPS" ~ "CPS",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_PAPCHILD" ~ "PAPCHILD",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_PAPFAM" ~ "PAPFAM",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_GFHS" ~ "GFHS",
                                 TRUE ~ Data_Source)) %>%
  distinct(Country_name, Data_Source_g, Data_Source, cohort_c) %>%
  group_by(Country_name, Data_Source_g) %>%
  summarise(n_points_source_g = n(),
            Data_Source_names = paste(sort(unique(Data_Source)), collapse = ", "),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(Country_name) %>%
  summarise(n_Data_Source_g_country = n_distinct(Data_Source_g),
            Data_Source_g_country_list = paste(Data_Source_g,
                                               n_points_source_g, sep = " = ", collapse = ", "),
            Data_source = paste(sort(unique(Data_Source_names)), collapse = ", "),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  select(Country_name, Data_Source_g_country_list, Data_source)




#Some data manipulation for conformity
agefb$parameter <- "Age at first birth"
agefb <- agefb %>%
  mutate(Country_name = as.factor(Country_name),
         sex = as.factor(sex),
         Data_Source_g = if_else(Data_Source %in% c("Census - published", "Census - CFE", "Census - IPUMS", "Census"), "Census",
                                 if_else(Data_Source %in% c("HFD", "Other published"), "Other",
                                         if_else(Data_Source == "Register", "Register", "Survey"))),
         Data_Source_g = as.factor(Data_Source_g),
         across(is.numeric, round, digits = 2))


#Create the dataset for the sources with the respective country name in afb
ds_g_country_flag_afb <- agefb %>%
  mutate(Data_Source = case_when(Data_Source == "Survey_National Statistics" ~ "National Statistics surveys",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_WFS" ~ "WFS",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_FFS" ~ "FFS",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_CPS" ~ "CPS",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_PAPCHILD" ~ "PAPCHILD",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_PAPFAM" ~ "PAPFAM",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_GFHS" ~ "GFHS",
                                 TRUE ~ Data_Source)) %>%
  distinct(Country_name, Data_Source_g, Data_Source, Year) %>%
  group_by(Country_name, Data_Source_g) %>%
  summarise(n_points_source_g = n(),
            Data_Source_names = paste(sort(unique(Data_Source)), collapse = ", "),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(Country_name) %>%
  summarise(n_Data_Source_g_country = n_distinct(Data_Source_g),
             Data_Source_g_country_list = paste(Data_Source_g,
                                               n_points_source_g, sep = " = ", collapse = ", "),
            Data_source = paste(sort(unique(Data_Source_names)), collapse = ", "),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  select(Country_name, Data_Source_g_country_list, Data_source)



#Some data manipulation for conformity
asfrdhs$parameter <- "Age-specific fertility rate"
asfrdhs <- asfrdhs %>%
  rename(Country_name = locname,
             sex = gndr, 
         Data_Source = source,
         Year = year1)%>%
  mutate(Country_name = as.factor(Country_name),
         sex = if_else(sex == "F", "female","male"),
         sex = as.factor(sex),
         Data_Source_g = "Survey",
         across(is.numeric, round, digits = 2),
         Year = if_else(Country_name == "Nepal", Year-56, Year),
         Year = round(Year, 0))

#Dummy completed cohort or tfr data (replace with actual later)
tfrdhs <- asfrdhs %>%
  group_by(Country_name, Year, sex, survey, Data_Source, Data_Source_g) %>%
  summarise(tfr = sum(rate, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")

tfrdhs$parameter <- "Total fertility rate"

#Create the dataset for the sources with the respective country name in frs
ds_g_country_flag_asfr <- asfrdhs %>%
  mutate(Data_Source = case_when(Data_Source == "Survey_National Statistics" ~ "National Statistics surveys",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_WFS" ~ "WFS",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_FFS" ~ "FFS",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_CPS" ~ "CPS",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_PAPCHILD" ~ "PAPCHILD",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_PAPFAM" ~ "PAPFAM",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_GFHS" ~ "GFHS",
                                 TRUE ~ Data_Source)) %>%
  distinct(Country_name, Data_Source_g, Data_Source, Year) %>%
  group_by(Country_name, Data_Source_g) %>%
  summarise(n_points_source_g = n(),
            Data_Source_names = paste(sort(unique(Data_Source)), collapse = ", "),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(Country_name) %>%
  summarise(n_Data_Source_g_country = n_distinct(Data_Source_g),
            Data_Source_g_country_list = paste(Data_Source_g,
                                               n_points_source_g, sep = " = ", collapse = ", "),
            Data_source = paste(sort(unique(Data_Source_names)), collapse = ", "),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  select(Country_name, Data_Source_g_country_list, Data_source)



#Now make the variables available for the app, like the country names and data sources for now

datasets <- list(smoothed_data_all_ds_hfd, agefb, asfrdhs, tfrdhs)

countries <- datasets %>%
  lapply(\(x) as.character(unique(x$Country_name))) %>%
  unlist() %>%
  unique() %>%
  sort()

parameters <- datasets %>%
  lapply(\(x) as.character(unique(x$parameter))) %>%
  unlist() %>%
  unique() %>%
  sort() 


data <- as.character(unique(smoothed_data_all_ds_hfd$Data_Source))

#Create the dataset for the sources with the respective country name 

ds_all <- smoothed_data_all_ds_hfd %>%
  full_join(agefb) %>%
  full_join(asfrdhs) %>%
  full_join(tfrdhs) 

ds_g_country_flag_all <- unique(ds_all %>%
  select(Country_name, Data_Source, parameter)) %>%
  mutate(Data_Source_g = if_else(Data_Source %in% c("Census - published", "Census - CFE", "Census - IPUMS", "Census"), "Census",
                                 if_else(Data_Source %in% c("HFD", "Other published"), "Other",
                                         if_else(Data_Source == "Register", "Register", "Survey"))),
         Data_Source = case_when(Data_Source == "Survey_National Statistics" ~ "National Statistics surveys",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_WFS" ~ "WFS",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_FFS" ~ "FFS",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_CPS" ~ "CPS",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_PAPCHILD" ~ "PAPCHILD",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_PAPFAM" ~ "PAPFAM",
                                 Data_Source == "Survey_GFHS" ~ "GFHS",
                                 TRUE ~ Data_Source))  %>%
  distinct(Country_name, Data_Source_g, Data_Source, parameter) %>%
  group_by(Country_name, Data_Source_g, parameter) %>%
  summarise(n_points_source_g = n(),
            Data_Source_names = paste(sort(unique(Data_Source)), collapse = ", "),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(Country_name, parameter) %>%
  summarise(n_Data_Source_g_country = n_distinct(Data_Source_g),
            Data_Source_g_country_list = paste(Data_Source_g,
                                               n_points_source_g, sep = " = ", collapse = ", "),
            Data_source = paste(sort(unique(Data_Source_names)), collapse = ", "),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  select(Country_name, Data_Source_g_country_list, Data_source, parameter)



#Country parameter for the childlessness page
countries_childlessness <- smoothed_data_all_ds_hfd %>%
  pull(Country_name) %>%
  as.character() %>%
  unique() %>%
  sort()


#Country parameter for the age at first birth page
countries_afb <- agefb %>%
  pull(Country_name) %>%
  as.character() %>%
  unique() %>%
  sort()


#Country parameter for the fertility rate page
datasets_fr <- list(asfrdhs, tfrdhs)

countries_fr <- datasets_fr %>%
  lapply(\(x) as.character(unique(x$Country_name))) %>%
  unlist() %>%
  unique() %>%
  sort()

parameters_fr <- datasets_fr %>%
  lapply(\(x) as.character(unique(x$parameter))) %>%
  unlist() %>%
  unique() %>%
  sort() 

year_fr <- datasets_fr %>%
  lapply(\(x) as.character(unique(x$Year))) %>%
  unlist() %>%
  unique() %>%
  sort() 

year_max_fr <- datasets_fr %>%
  lapply(\(x) {
    x %>%
      filter(!is.na(Country_name), !is.na(Year)) %>%
      group_by(Country_name) %>%
      summarise(Year_max = max(Year, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop")
  }) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  group_by(Country_name) %>%
  summarise(Year_max = max(Year_max, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(asfrdhs, by = "Country_name") %>%
  mutate(Recent = if_else(Year == Year_max, "Recent", "Previous surveys"),
         Year = round(Year, 0))

#Make the year parameters for the home page
year <- datasets_fr %>%
  lapply(\(x) {
    if (!"Year" %in% names(x)) {
      return(numeric(0))
    }
    
    round(as.numeric(x$Year), 0)
  }) %>%
  unlist() %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  as.character() %>%
  unique() %>%
  sort()

year_max <- datasets_fr %>%
  lapply(\(x) {
    x %>%
      filter(!is.na(Country_name), !is.na(Year)) %>%
      group_by(Country_name) %>%
      summarise(Year_max = max(Year, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop")
  }) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  group_by(Country_name) %>%
  summarise(Year_max = max(Year_max, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(asfrdhs, by = "Country_name") %>%
  mutate(Recent = if_else(Year == Year_max, "Recent", "Previous surveys"),
         Year = round(Year, 0))


# Map parameter names to plot output IDs
plot_map <- c("Childlessness" = "lineplot", "Age at first birth" = "lineplot2", "Age-specific fertility rate" = "lineplot3", 
              "Total fertility rate" = "lineplot4")

plot_map_fr <- c("Age-specific fertility rate" = "lineplot_asfr", "Total fertility rate" = "lineplot_tfr")


#This ui is for the password!
login_overlay <- tags$div(
  id = "login_overlay",
  
  tags$div(
    class = "login-card",
    
    h2("World Fertility Database"),
    p("Please enter the password to access the app."),
    
    passwordInput(inputId = "app_password", label = "Password"),
    
    checkboxInput(inputId = "show_password", label = "Show password", value = FALSE),
    
    actionButton(inputId = "login", label = "Open database", class = "btn-primary"),
    
    uiOutput("login_message")
  )
)

#We now build the user interface for the Shiny page
main_ui <- page_navbar(title = div(class = "app-brand", 
                              div("World Fertility Database", class = "app-brand-title"),
  div("A Comparative Interactive Fertility Outcome Platform for Males and Females", class = "app-brand-subtitle")),
                  id = "main_nav",
                  navbar_options = navbar_options(position = "static-top"), fluid = TRUE, fillable = FALSE,
  
  nav_panel(title = "Home", value = "fert",
            div(class = "about-wrap",
                div(class = "home-grid",
                    div(class = "home-card",
               div(class = "home-intro",
                            h2("Explore fertility outcomes by sex, country, and time", class = "home-title"),
                            p("The World Fertility Database brings together harmonised estimates of male and female fertility outcomes from multiple data sources. Use the controls on the left to compare countries and indicators, view the corresponding plots, and download selected data from the outcome-specific pages.",
                              class = "home-lead"))),
            
            div(class = "home-grids",
                    div(class = "home-card",
                        h2("Over 170 countries", class = "home-mini-title"),
                        p("The Database covers over 170 countries across the various fertility outcomes.", style = "text-align: justify;")),
               
               div(class = "home-card",
                   h2("4 fertility outcomes", class = "home-mini-title"),
                   p("Discover four broad fertility outcomes across gender and different periods and cohorts.", style = "text-align: justify;"))
               )),
           
            div(class = "home-grid",
                div(class = "home-card",
                    h3("Fertility outcomes"),
                    p("The Database provides a data and graphics for male and female childlessness proportions, age at first birth, ages-specific and total fertility rates.
                      The four outcomes are graphically presented together", actionLink("go_overview", "here."), "Invididual pages dedicated to each fertility outcome can be viewed under the Fertility Explorer tab.", style = "text-align: left;"),
                    
                    tags$div(lang = "en", style = "display: flex; gap: 30px; align-items: flex-start; text-align: left; hyphens: auto; overflow-wrap: break-word; line-height: 1.6;",
                      tags$div(style = "flex: 1;",
                                
                      p(h4("Childlessness"),
                        p("Childlessness proportions are provided for men and women across cohorts specific to each country.", actionLink("go_fert_chness", tags$img(src = "chness.png",
                          style = "width: 100%; max-width: 800px; cursor: pointer;")), style = "text-align: left; hyphens: auto; overflow-wrap: break-word; line-height: 1.6;")),
                      
                      p(h4("Age at first birth"),
                        p("The age at first birth for men and women data provided are for men and women over various periods per country.",
                          actionLink("go_fert_afb", tags$img(src = "afb.png", style = "width: 100%; max-width: 800px; cursor: pointer;")), style = "text-align: left; hyphens: auto; overflow-wrap: break-word; line-height: 1.6;"))
                      ),
                       br(),
                       br(),
                      tags$div(
                        style = "flex: 1;",
                        p(h4("Age-specific fertility rate"),
                          p("In each country where data is available, the database shows the age-specific fertility rates (ASFR) for men and women across periods.", 
                            actionLink("go_fert_fr", tags$img(src = "asfr.png", style = "width: 100%; max-width: 800px; cursor: pointer;")), style = "text-align: left; hyphens: auto; overflow-wrap: break-word; line-height: 1.6;")),
                        
                        p(h4("Total fertility rate"),
                          p("The total fertility rates are subsequently calculated for men and women over various periods per country using the ASFRs.",
                            actionLink("go_fert_fr", tags$img(src = "tfr.png", style = "width: 100%; max-width: 800px; cursor: pointer;")), style = "text-align: left; hyphens: auto; overflow-wrap: break-word; line-height: 1.6;"))
                        )
                )
                )
            ))

            
  ),
  
  nav_panel(title = "About", value = "use",
            
           div(class = "about-wrap",
                div(class = "about-intro",
                    div(class = "about-kicker", "World Fertility Database"),
                    h2("About the database", class = "about-title"),
                    fluidRow(column(width = 12, p("The", actionLink("go_overview", "Fertility Overview"), "page shows fertility outcome plots by gender. The plots allow users to compare male and female fertility outcomes such as childlessness proportions, fertility rates, and age at first birth across countries. Detailed plots and data can be found under the respective fertility explorer pages.",
                                                  class = "about-lead")))
                ),
                
                div(class = "about-grid",
                    div(class = "about-card",
                        h3("How to use the data"),
                        p("Plots can be downloaded using the camera icon in the plot toolbar, and data can be downloaded using the Download table button. By checking 'Download all available data' and clicking the Download table button, all datasets in the database for the specific fertility outcome can be downloaded."),
                        p("To download selected countries and variables of interest, choose the variable in the side panel and click on the Download table button. All datasets include male and female estimates.")
                    ),
                    
                    div(class = "about-card",
                        h3("What to pay attention to"),
                        p("When using the data, users should pay attention to the indicator, sex, country, time variable, estimate type, and data source."),
                        p("For childlessness, the downloadable tables include both original estimates and smoothed GAM estimates. For the other fertility outcomes, the current tables include original estimates from specific data sources.")
                    )
                ),
                
                div(class = "about-grid",
                    div(class = "about-card", h3("Using the data"),
                        p("Because estimates are drawn from different types of data sources, comparisons across countries and time should be interpreted with attention to data availability, source type, and estimation method. More information on the construction of each indicator is provided in the Methods section."),
                        p("Users should cite or acknowledge the World Fertility Database when using downloaded data or plots in publications, presentations, or other outputs."),
                        p("Learn more about the Global Male Fertility Database project ",
                          tags$a("here.", href = "https://sites.uclouvain.be/male-fertility/?Project", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer"))
                    ),
                    
                    div(class = "about-card", h3("Team"),
                        div(class = "person-box",
                            div(class = "person-role", "Head"),
                            tags$a("Bruno Schoumaker", href = "https://www.uclouvain.be/en/people/bruno.schoumaker", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer")),
                        
                        div(class = "person-box",
                            div(class = "person-role", "Researchers"),
                            tags$span("Chaimae Drioui"),
                            br(),
                            tags$a("David Sánchez-Paéz", href = "https://investigacion.uva.es/SPPC/researcher?lang=en&researcherId=409103", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer"),
                            br(),
                            tags$span("Afua Durowaa-Boateng")
                        )
                    )
                ),
                
                div(class = "about-software", h3("Software"),
                    p("This web application was built using R and the open-source R packages below."),
                    
                    tags$ul(tags$li(tags$a("shiny", href = "https://doi.org/10.32614/CRAN.package.shiny", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer"),
                                    ": Chang W, Cheng J, Allaire J, Sievert C, Schloerke B, Aden-Buie G, Xie, Allen, McPherson, Dipert, Borges (2026). shiny: Web Application Framework for R. R package version 1.13.0."),
                            
                            tags$li(tags$a("bslib", href = "https://doi.org/10.32614/CRAN.package.bslib", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer"),
                                    ": Sievert C, Cheng J, Aden-Buie G (2026). bslib: Custom 'Bootstrap' 'Sass' Themes for 'shiny' and 'rmarkdown'. R package version 0.10.0."),
                            
                            tags$li(tags$a("tidyverse", href = "https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.01686", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer"),
                                    ": Wickham et al. (2019). Welcome to the tidyverse. Journal of Open Source Software, 4(43), 1686."),
                            
                            tags$li(tags$a("ggplot2", href = "https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer"),
                                    ": H. Wickham (2016). ggplot2: Elegant Graphics for Data Analysis. Springer-Verlag New York."),
                            
                            tags$li(tags$a("DT", href = "https://doi.org/10.32614/CRAN.package.DT", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer"),
                                    ": Xie Y, Cheng J, Tan X, Aden-Buie G (2025). DT: A Wrapper of the JavaScript Library 'DataTables'. R package version 0.34.0."),
                            
                            tags$li(tags$a("ggokabeito", href = "https://doi.org/10.32614/CRAN.package.ggokabeito", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer"),
                                    ":Barrett M (2021) ggokabeito: 'Okabe-Ito' Scales for 'ggplot2' and 'ggraph'. R package version 0.1.0.")
                    )
                ))
            
  ), 
  
  nav_panel(title = "Fertility Overview", value = "overview",
            layout_sidebar(
              fill = FALSE,
              sidebar = sidebar(
                class = "sticky-sidebar", "Controls", width = "320px",
                br(),
                div(class = "sidebar-tip", tags$strong("Tip: "),
                    "Select up to four countries for clearer plots. Due to the limitations of the colour palette used in the graphs, 9 unique countries at can be selected at a time."), 
                
                
                selectInput("Country","Choose one or more countries",
                            choices = countries,
                            selected = c("Austria", "Belgium", "France", "Ghana"), multiple = TRUE, width = "100%" ), 
                
                
                selectInput("Variable","Choose one or more fertility outcomes",
                            choices = parameters,
                            selected = c("Age at first birth","Childlessness"), multiple = TRUE, width = "100%"),
                
                
                selectInput("year","Choose one or more periods ONLY for ASFR plot",
                            choices = year,
                            selected = c(1991, 2020), multiple = TRUE, width = "100%"),
                
                
                checkboxInput("most_recent", "View the most recent survey ONLY for ASFR plot", value = FALSE),
              ),
              fluidRow(column(width = 12, 
                              p("The childlessness plots include both original estimates and smoothed GAM estimates. The other fertility outcome plots currently show original estimates from specific data sources. Plots can be downloaded using the camera icon in the plot toolbar. Learn more about the World Fertility Database project and using the database ",
                                tags$a("here.",
                                       href = "#", onclick = "document.querySelector('a[data-value=\"use\"]').click(); return false;"), style = "text-align: justify;")
              )),
              uiOutput("selected_plots"),
              br()
            )
            
  ),
  
  nav_menu(title = "Fertility Explorer", 
           nav_panel(title = "Childlessness", value = "fert_chness",
                     fluidRow(column(width = 12,  div(class = "chness-brand", 
                                                      div("Fertility outcome", class = "chness-brand-title"),
                                                      div("Childlessness", class = "chness-brand-subtitle"),
                                                      p("Here are childlessness proportions for males and females across multiple countries. 
                                  The childlessness plot shows a fitted GAM together with the original estimates from various data sources. The GAM shows the smoothed estimates.
                                           Learn more about the data and methods ", actionLink("go_chness", "here."),
                                                   " Download all or selected childlessness data below.")))),
                     
                     layout_sidebar(
                       fill = FALSE,
                       sidebar = sidebar(
                         class = "sticky-sidebar", "Controls", width = "320px",
                         
                                         br(),
                                         div(class = "sidebar-tip", tags$strong("Tip: "),
                                             "Select up to four countries for clearer plots. You can still download data for larger country selections."),
                                         br(),
                                         selectInput("Country_childlessness","Choose one or more countries",
                                                     choices = countries_childlessness,
                                                     selected = c("Austria", "Belgium"), multiple = TRUE, width = "100%" ), 
                              
                                         br()
                                         
                       ),
                       div(class = "adaptive-plot", plotlyOutput("lineplot_childlessness", height = "100%")),
                       br(),
                       fluidRow(column(width = 12, div(class = "selected-data-toolbar", h4("Selected childlessness data", class = "selected-data-heading"),
                                                       div(class = "download-all-wrap",tags$input(id = "download_all_childlessness", type = "checkbox", class = "download-all-checkbox"), 
                                                           tags$label(`for` = "download_all_childlessness", "Download all available childlessness data", class = "download-all-label")),
                                                       downloadButton("download_selected_table_childlessness", "Download table", class = "btn btn-default download-table-button")),
                                       
                                       DTOutput("selected_table_childlessness", width = "100%")))
                     )),
           nav_panel(title = "Age at first birth", value = "fert_afb",
                     fluidRow(column(width = 12, div(class = "afb-brand", 
                                                     div("Fertility outcome", class = "afb-brand-title"),
                                                     div("Age at first birth", class = "afb-brand-subtitle"),
                                     p("These plots show age at first birth for males and females across multiple countries and time periods. 
                                  The plot shows the original estimates from various data sources. 
                                          Learn more about age at first birth data sources and methods ", actionLink("go_chness", "here"),
                                                   ". Download all or selected age at first birth data below.")))),
                     
                     layout_sidebar(
                       fill = FALSE,
                       sidebar = sidebar(
                         class = "sticky-sidebar", "Controls", width = "320px",
                                         
                                         br(),
                                         div(class = "sidebar-tip", tags$strong("Tip: "),
                                             "Select up to four countries for clearer plots. You can still download data for larger country selections."),
                                         br(),
                                         selectInput("Country_afb","Choose one or more countries",
                                                     choices = countries_afb,
                                                     selected = c("Afghanistan", "Ghana"), multiple = TRUE, width = "100%" ),
                                         br()
                                         
                            ),
                       div(class = "adaptive-plot", plotlyOutput("lineplot_afb", height = "100%")),
                       br(),

#This is a helper function to make sure my plotlys have the right legends
`%||%` <- function(x, y) {
  if (is.null(x)) y else x
}

escape_regex <- function(x) {
  gsub("([][{}()+*^$|\\\\?.])", "\\\\\\1", x)
}

get_country_from_trace_name <- function(nm, country_levels) {
  nm <- nm %||% ""
  nm <- gsub("^\\((.*)\\)$", "\\1", nm)
  
  hits <- country_levels[
    vapply(country_levels,
           function(cty) {
             grepl(paste0("^", escape_regex(cty), "(,|$)"), nm)
           },
           logical(1)
    )
  ]
  
  if (length(hits) > 0) {
    hits[which.max(nchar(hits))]
  } else {
    nm
  }
}

make_shape_maps <- function(data, shape_var = "Data_Source_g") {
  
  shape_levels <- intersect(c("Census", "Survey", "Register", "Other"), sort(unique(na.omit(as.character(data[[shape_var]])))))
  
  gg_shape_values <- c("Census" = 15, "Survey" = 4, "Register" = 17,"Other" = 18)
                       # square, x / cross, triangle-up, diamond
  
  plotly_symbol_values <- c("Census"   = "square",  "Survey"   = "x", "Register" = "triangle-up", "Other"    = "diamond")
  
  list(levels = shape_levels,  gg = gg_shape_values[shape_levels], plotly = plotly_symbol_values[shape_levels])
}

plotly_country_source_legend <- function(
    p,
    gg_obj,
    data,
    x_var,
    y_var,
    country_var = "Country_name",
    source_var = NULL,
    source_plotly_symbols = NULL,
    show_source_legend = !is.null(source_var),
    source_label_prefix = "") {
  
  country_levels <- sort(unique(na.omit(as.character(data[[country_var]]))))
  
  gb <- ggplot_build(gg_obj)
  colour_scale <- gb$plot$scales$get_scales("colour")
  
  country_cols <- setNames(colour_scale$map(country_levels),country_levels)
  
  has_lines <- any(vapply(
    p$x$data,
    function(tr) {
      mode <- tr$mode %||% ""
      fill <- tr$fill %||% "none"
      grepl("lines", mode) && identical(fill, "none")
    },
    logical(1)
  ))
  
  seen_countries <- character()
  
  for (i in seq_along(p$x$data)) {
    
    nm   <- p$x$data[[i]]$name %||% ""
    mode <- p$x$data[[i]]$mode %||% ""
    fill <- p$x$data[[i]]$fill %||% "none"
    
    is_line <- grepl("lines", mode) && identical(fill, "none")
    is_ribbon <- !identical(fill, "none")
    
    cty <- get_country_from_trace_name(nm, country_levels)
    
    p$x$data[[i]]$name <- cty
    p$x$data[[i]]$legendgroup <- paste0("country_", cty)
    
    if (has_lines && is_line && !is_ribbon) {
      p$x$data[[i]]$showlegend <- !(cty %in% seen_countries)
      seen_countries <- c(seen_countries, cty)
    } else {
      p$x$data[[i]]$showlegend <- FALSE
    }
  }
  
  x0 <- suppressWarnings(min(data[[x_var]], na.rm = TRUE))
  y0 <- suppressWarnings(min(data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE))
  
  if (!is.finite(x0)) x0 <- 0
  if (!is.finite(y0)) y0 <- 0
  
  # If there are no line traces, create a manual country legend.
  if (!has_lines) {
    for (cty in country_levels) {
      p <- add_trace(p, x = x0, y = y0, type = "scatter", mode = "markers",
        marker = list(color = country_cols[[cty]], symbol = "circle", size = 9),
        name = cty, legendgroup = paste0("country_", cty), showlegend = TRUE, visible = "legendonly", hoverinfo = "skip", inherit = FALSE)
    }
  }
  
  # Optional data-source shape legend.
  if (show_source_legend && !is.null(source_var) && source_var %in% names(data)) {
    
    source_levels <- make_shape_maps(data, source_var)$levels
    
    if (length(source_levels) > 0) {
      
      if (is.null(source_plotly_symbols)) {
        source_plotly_symbols <- make_shape_maps(data, source_var)$plotly
      }
      
      for (src in source_levels) {
        p <- add_trace(p, x = x0, y = y0, type = "scatter", mode = "markers",
          marker = list(symbol = source_plotly_symbols[[src]], color = "black", size = 9),
          name = paste0(source_label_prefix, src), legendgroup = paste0("source_", src),
          showlegend = TRUE, visible = "legendonly", hoverinfo = "skip", inherit = FALSE)
      }
    }
  }
  
  p %>%
    layout(legend = list(title = list(text = "")))
}

#This is a helper function for the error message

validate_okabe_ito <- function(variable) {
  
  number_of_groups <- length(unique(variable[!is.na(variable)]))
  
  maximum_groups <- length(ggokabeito::palette_okabe_ito())
  
  shiny::validate(
    shiny::need(number_of_groups <= maximum_groups,
      sprintf(paste0("Okabe–Ito palette supports a maximum of 9 countries at a time. ", "Please reduce the number of selected countries."),
        number_of_groups,maximum_groups)),
    errorClass = "palette-limit"
  )
}

    datatable(table_data_fr,  class = "stripe hover compact my-dt", options = list(pageLength = 10, scrollX = TRUE, searching = FALSE, lengthChange = FALSE, dom = "tip"), rownames = FALSE, filter = "top")
  })
  
  
  output$download_selected_table_fr <- downloadHandler(
    
    filename = function() {
      
      if (isTRUE(input$download_all_fr)) {
        paste0("all_fr_data_", Sys.Date(), ".csv")
      } else {
        paste0("selected_fr_data_", Sys.Date(), ".csv")
      }
    },
    
    content = function(file) {
      write.csv(download_table_data_fr(), file, row.names = FALSE)
    }
  )
  
  

}

# Run the application 
shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)
